status: DRAFT — NOT REGISTERED. Design notes only; src/framework/index.ts deliberately does not load this file.
description: DRAFT. Defeasible deontic logic over normative texts — model a statute, code of ethics, or rule-book as passages, then QUERY it: what is obligatory here, what is permitted, which rule wins when two conflict. The modeling half is expressible with today's machinery (rules are snippets; norms are snippet→concept tags; defeat is rule→rule, plain binary LINK — no reification needed because rules are entities). The QUERY half is deliberately out of scope for the framework file: it is an enrich-side ADAPTER (implementation_plan_mvp.md, Source Adapters) that exports the tagged subgraph as an answer-set program (clingo), runs the solver, and returns answer sets as view-only candidates — precedence user > solver, never authoritative writes. Translation sketch: each #Obliges/#Permits/#Forbids edge becomes a defeasible rule head obliged(Act) etc.; #ExceptionTo becomes an exception literal in the defeated rule's body; #Defeats becomes a superiority relation (as in Gordon/Prakken-style formalisms). BLOCKED ON: F1 (lint: a #Defeats cycle, an exception targeting a non-rule) and F2 (hooks: run-solver-on-change) — ROADMAP §2.4 — plus the adapter runtime. Do not register until at least the lint half exists; served vocabulary users can tag with but nothing checks is a trap.

edgeTags.name: Obliges
edgeTags.description: DRAFT — WRONG SHAPE ON PURPOSE, see notes: a norm passage obliging an act-concept wants snippet→concept, but CLARIFIES/CONTRADICTS are polarity primitives and ABOUT excludes snippets. Registering this framework requires deciding the norm→act edge: extend ABOUT to snippet→concept, or make norms concepts. This unresolved selector is the main open design question

edgeTags.name: Defeats
edgeTags.description: DRAFT: this rule-passage takes priority over that one when both apply (lex specialis, lex superior — the superiority relation the solver consumes)

edgeTags.name: ExceptionTo
edgeTags.description: DRAFT: this passage carves out a case where that rule does not apply — compiles to an exception literal in the target rule's body, not a contrary conclusion (the defeasible-logic distinction between undercutting and rebutting)
